parsed_review.summary: CP00-744 (RP24 Korean Legal Depth P06 Permission Fixture Bridge, risk A, 10 units RP24.P06.M05.S17-RP24.P06.M06.S04) is a sound descriptor-only closeout pack. Pack binding (registry.js:383), requirements (registry.js:3387), manifest, baseline, queue (live_latest_pack_id=CP00-744), and the generated contract all agree on identity, range, upstream CP00-743, next CP00-745, and deviation_from_plan=false. Deliverable distribution {security_audit:3,test:4,implementation:3} and micro-title distribution {Permission And Audit Binding:6,Synthetic Fixture Set:4} match the prompt and the contract validation block (cp744 coverage+descriptor valid:true, errors:[] at contract line 64975/65057). The descriptor chains correctly from CP743 (service.js:99-100); index.js re-exports all six required public symbols; README #cp00-744 is accurate; the validate-rp24 generator (scripts/validate-rp24-korean-legal-depth-contract.mjs:34-38) asserts coverage/descriptor errors are empty before writing the contract. No-write/no-real-data/no-runtime boundaries are enforced in code (KOREAN_LEGAL_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION, validateDescriptor no-leak guards) and reflected in the actual case-set rows (contract 29222+). Authority boundary is correct: claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false; runtime_opening_pack_id is deferred to CP00-745, consistent with runtime_ready=false. Hermes is used only as a deterministic control-plane evidence/receipt shape (emits_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. Manifest correctly holds production_ready=false pending this review (no overclaim); adjudication/inspection are consistent. Hardening checks pass: readiness auth_preflight succeeded (exit 0, 1445B valid JSON), tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob, no forbidden tools, summary-only correctly prohibited, and no prior invalid review evidence exists. Claude acted strictly as read-only reviewer (no Bash/edits) and is not the final approver; enterprise-trust/runtime approval remains a human authority outside this receipt and is not claimed by this pack, so closing this CP is not blocked. The descriptor-verified production_ready claim is supported; recommend PASS for pack closeout.

findings.title: Review evidence basis is recorded harness output plus static corroboration, not reviewer re-execution
findings.details: As a read-only reviewer (Read/Grep/Glob, no Bash), I did not re-execute the validation commands. PASS rests on (a) the recorded command-evidence.json (all 24 commands exit_code 0, including npm test, npm run validate, rp24:korean-legal-depth:validate, closeout-pack:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) and (b) my own direct static inspection of the regenerated contract validation block (cp744 coverage+descriptor valid:true, errors:[]), the registry/service/validators sources, tests, and no-write attestation. This is the intended read_tools_used inspection mode, not a defect in the pack.
findings.recommendation: None required. The closeout harness (not the reviewer) is the authority for live command execution; the recorded evidence and statically-corroborated contract are mutually consistent. No action needed before closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational; does not block pack or goal closeout. Provenance transparency only.
